title: ReactionTarget
description: A ReactionTarget addresses a single handler invocation for a single subject. A classifier's `ClassifyResult` carries any number per classified event. The handler-log records `{ts, agent, handler, target_id, debounce_key, payload}` for each dispatched ReactionTarget; consumers observing the log validate against this schema.

handler.description: Name of the registered handler to dispatch to. Registered handlers live in `app/Bridge/Handlers/` (or a custom handler the agent registers). Conventional shape: lowercase, underscore-separated (e.g., `log_intent`, `notify_channel_mcp`).

target_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject being addressed. Often duplicates the source Intent's `subject_id` but may differ when one Intent fans out to multiple targets (e.g., a cross-board move addresses both source and dest boards as separate targets).

debounce_key.description: Coalescing/correlation key for handlers and consumers. The synchronous bridge dispatches every ReactionTarget and does NOT itself coalesce or suppress by `debounce_key` (no drain pass, no cross-delivery tracker); it is recorded in the handler-log for consumers that choose to coalesce. Conventional shape: `<resource>:<id>:<action>` (e.g., `task:42:any`).

debounce_seconds.description: Advisory per-target debounce window for the consumer to honor; the synchronous bridge carries it but does not enforce a cross-delivery window. `null` defers to the consumer's default. Present-but-null is the conventional 'inherit default' marker.

payload.description: Handler-specific structured data. Schema is per-`handler`. A plain JSON object on the wire (a plain associative array in memory; no freeze/thaw).
